edsamplr 0.0.0.9000
  • Get started
  • Reference

    Generate samples of correlated quantitative data

    generate_correlated.Rd

    generate_correlated() is a function to generate samples of correlated quantitative data with specified first four moments and specified slope.

    generate_correlated(
  n = 100,
  mean = 0,
  var = 1,
  skew = 0,
  kurt = 0,
  slope = 1,
  r = 0.5,
  summary = TRUE,
  decimals = 3,
  replication = 1
)

    Arguments

    n

    A numeric vector for the sample size. The default value is 100.

    mean

    A numeric vector for the population mean of the first variable. The default value is 0.

    var

    A numeric vector for the population variance of the first variable. The default value is 1.

    skew

    A numeric vector for the population skew of the variables. Separate values of skew can be entered. The default value is 0.

    kurt

    A numeric vector for the population kurtosis of the variables. Separate values of kurtosis can be entered. The default value is 0.

    slope

    A numeric vector for the slope of the regression line. The default value is 0.

    r

    A numeric vector for the correlation between the variables. The default value is 0.5.

    summary

    A logical vector for whether the return should include summary statistics. The default value is TRUE.

    decimals

    A numeric vector for the number of decimals to round the sample data to. The default value is 3.

    replication

    A numeric vector for the number of times to replicate the sampling. The default value is 1.

    Value

    If summary = TRUE, a list containing the correlation of the sample, the slope of the sample, a data frame ("sample") with n rows and 2 columns, and a matrix ("summary") of the input parameters and descriptive statistics. If summary=FALSE, a data frame with n rows and 2 columns.

    Examples

    generate_correlated()
#> $correlation
#> [1] 0.518
#> 
#> $slope
#> [1] 0.885
#> 
#> $summary
#>          mean   sd  skew kurtosis
#> input 1  0.00 1.00  0.00     0.00
#> k 1     -0.07 1.04 -0.09     0.10
#> input 2  0.00 2.00  0.00     0.00
#> k 2     -0.20 1.77  0.00     0.46
#> 
#> $sample
#>         X1     X2
#> 1   -2.364 -0.582
#> 2   -0.560 -0.328
#> 3   -0.987 -1.255
#> 4    0.482 -1.430
#> 5    0.369  3.855
#> 6   -0.878 -1.180
#> 7    0.544  1.175
#> 8   -2.490 -0.074
#> 9    1.474  1.138
#> 10  -2.024 -0.952
#> 11   1.168  1.463
#> 12   1.289 -0.160
#> 13  -0.734 -1.477
#> 14  -0.981 -1.782
#> 15   0.095 -0.329
#> 16   0.830  1.100
#> 17  -0.501 -0.682
#> 18   1.087  1.083
#> 19  -0.231 -0.115
#> 20  -1.371 -1.105
#> 21  -1.674  0.001
#> 22   0.482 -0.007
#> 23   1.671 -0.234
#> 24  -0.674 -2.582
#> 25  -0.456 -2.987
#> 26  -1.176 -2.611
#> 27  -0.947  0.221
#> 28   0.149  2.599
#> 29   0.401  1.901
#> 30  -0.465  0.388
#> 31   0.409  1.264
#> 32   0.164 -3.019
#> 33   0.240  2.573
#> 34  -0.063  0.179
#> 35   0.163  0.437
#> 36  -1.096 -0.312
#> 37  -0.827 -2.534
#> 38   0.390  0.591
#> 39  -0.594 -1.365
#> 40  -0.575  0.638
#> 41   0.730 -0.291
#> 42  -0.624  0.296
#> 43   0.404 -0.486
#> 44   0.676  1.093
#> 45  -0.444 -1.187
#> 46  -1.769 -0.386
#> 47   0.541  0.724
#> 48   0.265  1.992
#> 49  -0.855 -2.479
#> 50   1.613  0.371
#> 51   0.565  0.433
#> 52  -0.092  2.934
#> 53  -0.131 -0.174
#> 54  -0.526 -2.125
#> 55  -0.039 -1.182
#> 56   0.543 -0.601
#> 57   0.675  0.032
#> 58   2.141  4.460
#> 59  -2.745 -1.993
#> 60  -0.145  1.766
#> 61   0.099 -1.449
#> 62  -0.244 -1.728
#> 63  -0.099  0.035
#> 64   0.803 -0.210
#> 65  -1.011 -0.135
#> 66   1.112  1.738
#> 67  -0.455  0.137
#> 68  -0.938 -3.776
#> 69  -1.598 -2.067
#> 70  -2.071 -3.100
#> 71  -0.849 -0.866
#> 72  -0.404  0.383
#> 73   1.078  1.301
#> 74   2.193  3.272
#> 75   2.358  3.482
#> 76  -2.099  0.521
#> 77   0.187 -0.226
#> 78   0.031 -2.740
#> 79   0.008 -2.737
#> 80  -0.094 -2.318
#> 81   0.884  0.550
#> 82   0.326 -1.482
#> 83  -0.606 -3.973
#> 84  -0.308 -1.373
#> 85  -0.643 -5.672
#> 86   0.399  0.560
#> 87   1.155 -0.423
#> 88  -0.209  0.540
#> 89   1.565  0.013
#> 90  -0.511 -0.236
#> 91   0.251  1.570
#> 92   0.401 -0.858
#> 93  -0.269 -1.237
#> 94  -0.948 -0.278
#> 95  -0.635 -0.883
#> 96  -0.655  1.729
#> 97   1.125  1.806
#> 98   0.384  0.744
#> 99   2.224  3.580
#> 100  0.900 -1.372
#> 

generate_correlated(skew = c(0.4, 0.25))
#> $correlation
#> [1] 0.463
#> 
#> $slope
#> [1] 0.899
#> 
#> $summary
#>          mean   sd skew kurtosis
#> input 1  0.00 1.00 0.40     0.00
#> k 1     -0.06 0.95 0.07     0.38
#> input 2  0.00 2.00 0.25     0.00
#> k 2     -0.23 1.86 0.11    -0.62
#> 
#> $sample
#>         X1     X2
#> 1    0.845  1.086
#> 2    0.762  0.847
#> 3    0.456  0.761
#> 4   -1.100 -2.017
#> 5    0.763 -2.242
#> 6    0.777 -0.254
#> 7   -1.064  0.282
#> 8   -0.536 -1.181
#> 9    2.894  3.535
#> 10   0.059 -0.892
#> 11  -0.830 -0.024
#> 12   0.372  0.797
#> 13  -1.721 -2.704
#> 14  -1.913 -2.446
#> 15   0.472  0.605
#> 16   0.876  0.761
#> 17  -0.118 -2.626
#> 18   0.897  1.201
#> 19   1.335  0.558
#> 20  -1.267 -1.956
#> 21   0.098  0.741
#> 22   0.607  0.391
#> 23   0.392 -1.942
#> 24   0.609 -2.099
#> 25  -0.098 -1.008
#> 26  -0.663 -2.568
#> 27  -0.017 -3.738
#> 28   1.939  3.877
#> 29  -0.699 -1.796
#> 30   0.479  0.378
#> 31  -0.581  0.761
#> 32  -0.644 -1.183
#> 33  -0.251 -4.263
#> 34  -0.328 -0.893
#> 35  -1.743 -2.113
#> 36  -0.657  0.960
#> 37  -1.449  0.232
#> 38  -0.786 -1.634
#> 39   0.599 -1.075
#> 40   1.276  1.723
#> 41   0.025  2.289
#> 42   0.768  0.140
#> 43  -1.260 -1.061

#> 45  -0.489 -1.602

#> 48  -0.495 -1.629

#> 52   0.610 -0.725
#> 53   0.271  0.058
#> 54   0.380  2.846

#> 57  -0.459 -1.160
#> 58   0.329  0.128
#> 59  -0.594  1.790
#> 60  -0.199 -2.949
#> 61   0.259 -0.559
#> 62   1.112  0.772
#> 63  -0.805  0.070
#> 64   0.737  1.678
#> 65  -0.971 -0.198
#> 66  -0.750 -3.469
#> 67  -0.955 -2.748
#> 68  -0.762 -3.050
#> 69  -0.706 -1.232
#> 70  -0.741 -0.312
#> 71   1.030  2.820
#> 72   1.397  3.321
#> 73   1.380  1.295
#> 74  -0.790  0.876
#> 75  -0.819  0.841
#> 76   0.453  1.675
#> 77  -0.371 -0.883
#> 78   0.661  2.914
#> 79   1.975  0.510
#> 80   0.383  1.370
#> 81  -0.738  1.193
#> 82   0.670 -0.442
#> 83   0.499 -0.342
#> 84  -0.606 -3.389
#> 85   1.391 -0.679
#> 86  -0.155  0.064
#> 87  -0.648  1.415
#> 88  -0.019 -2.315
#> 89  -1.278  0.890
#> 90  -0.237  0.257
#> 91  -1.633  0.485
#> 92   1.398  1.959
#> 93   0.200  4.065
#> 94   1.184  2.804
#> 95   0.318 -1.099
#> 96  -0.070 -2.094
#> 97   0.481 -1.800
#> 98  -1.761 -0.724
#> 99  -2.976 -1.356
#> 100 -1.064 -2.611
#> 

generate_correlated(slope = 3, r = 0.8)
#> $correlation
#> [1] 0.799
#> 
#> $slope
#> [1] 3.103
#> 
#> $summary
#>         mean   sd  skew kurtosis
#> input 1 0.00 1.00  0.00     0.00
#> k 1     0.18 0.99 -0.24     0.44
#> input 2 0.00 3.75  0.00     0.00
#> k 2     0.67 3.83 -0.38    -0.23
#> 
#> $sample
#>         X1      X2
#> 1   -0.134   1.909
#> 2   -0.156  -0.999
#> 3   -0.572   1.378
#> 4   -0.183  -1.166
#> 5   -1.838  -4.479
#> 6   -0.643  -1.461
#> 7    0.439  -1.236
#> 8    0.370  -0.371
#> 9    0.444  -3.564
#> 10  -0.440  -1.943
#> 11   0.088   3.705
#> 12  -0.419  -5.079
#> 13   0.197   4.205
#> 14   1.459   3.586
#> 15  -0.576  -2.877
#> 16   0.277   1.810

#> 24   0.979   5.561

#> 26  -0.011   0.691

#> 28   0.043   3.119

#> 30  -1.849  -7.947

#> 32   0.990   0.238
#> 33   0.319   4.662
#> 34  -0.255  -1.745
#> 35   1.423   5.233
#> 36  -0.017   2.788
#> 37   0.525  -1.069
#> 38   2.152   6.598
#> 39   1.158   2.616
#> 40   0.177  -3.619
#> 41   0.130  -0.065

#> 43   0.103  -0.170
#> 44   0.514   3.012
#> 45   1.198   3.348
#> 46   0.697   2.753

#> 50  -2.794  -9.027
#> 51  -1.118  -6.709
#> 52   0.122  -0.689
#> 53  -0.392  -3.657
#> 54   1.716   2.662
#> 55   0.197   0.365
#> 56   0.077   3.990
#> 57  -1.529  -3.169
#> 58   0.117  -2.155
#> 59   1.967   5.008
#> 60  -0.160   6.911
#> 61   0.453  -0.709
#> 62   0.506   0.309
#> 63   0.214   0.359
#> 64   0.917   5.318
#> 65   1.875   6.900
#> 66  -0.308   1.805
#> 67  -0.055  -0.013
#> 68  -0.227  -2.294
#> 69  -1.539  -4.092
#> 70   0.370   1.433
#> 71  -0.843  -1.524
#> 72  -0.228   3.784
#> 73   0.487   1.696
#> 74   0.549   2.625
#> 75   0.824   3.036
#> 76   0.204  -1.099
#> 77  -0.594   0.946
#> 78   0.782   1.245
#> 79   0.260   4.127
#> 80  -2.394 -10.392
#> 81   0.680   7.096
#> 82   0.377   3.021
#> 83   0.833   3.342
#> 84   0.603   3.031
#> 85  -0.357  -1.176
#> 86  -0.391  -0.938
#> 87   0.292   5.949
#> 88   0.091  -1.805
#> 89   0.239  -0.355
#> 90   1.268   5.413
#> 91  -0.970  -4.130
#> 92  -1.149  -3.246
#> 93   0.376   1.573
#> 94   1.218   0.903
#> 95   1.611   7.227
#> 96   0.767   5.052
#> 97  -0.573  -5.352
#> 98   1.789   3.928
#> 99   1.885   4.132
#> 100 -0.720  -2.646
#> 

generate_correlated(n = 10000, skew = c(0, 1), kurt = c(0, 1.5))
#> $correlation
#> [1] 0.492
#> 
#> $slope
#> [1] 0.986
#> 
#> $summary
#>          mean   sd  skew kurtosis
#> input 1  0.00 1.00  0.00     0.00
#> k 1     -0.01 0.99  0.03     1.00
#> input 2  0.00 2.00  1.00     1.50
#> k 2      0.01 1.99 -0.01     0.92
#> 
#> $sample
#>         X1     X2
#> 1   -0.398 -2.736
#> 2    0.040  0.969
#> 3   -1.411 -0.484
#> 4   -0.054  1.843
#> 5   -0.553  2.738
#> 6   -0.032  0.357
#> 7   -0.125 -0.458

#> 10   0.470  2.205

#> 25   0.036  1.273

#> 37   0.960  0.865

#> 200  0.951  0.497
#> 201  1.081  1.471
#> 202 -0.210 -0.337
#> 203 -0.901  0.765
#> 204 -0.804  0.687
#> 205 -0.826 -0.744
